reasoning:
The question asks whether between January 12, 2026, and May 1, 2026, the government of either Denmark or the U.S. will publicly acknowledge that the US has made an offer to purchase Greenland.

I've conducted extensive searches focusing specifically on the specified time range. The news indicates intense interest from the Trump administration in acquiring Greenland in early-to-mid January 2026, with instructions given to Secretary of State Marco Rubio to prepare a proposal. However, Denmark emphatically and repeatedly stated that Greenland is not for sale.

Crucially, reporting from late January (Jan 22-23) by the New York Times indicates that Trump reached a framework agreement focusing on "sovereign U.S. bases" and restrictions on Russian drilling, explicitly "stopping short" of transferring ownership of the island, and that Trump dropped his tariff threats against NATO allies.

As of April 12, 2026 (the forecast date), search results confirm that no formal offer has been made or acknowledged. Historical articles and deep-dives all conclude "no offer was ever made" (e.g., Time magazine on Feb 5). The Metaculus community was at 3% as of early April. With the explicit pivot to military bases and less than three weeks remaining until the deadline, the probability of an offer being made and acknowledged in the next 18 days is extremely low, and the probability it happened in the past and was missed is near 0. Therefore, I estimate a 2% chance for closure.
